body_txt: It is said that a rat will do anything to survive, no matter how disgraceful or dishonorable their tactics are. A resourceful rodent will rarely engage in head-on confrontations or fight fairly, because they know they can be easily overpowered. They must be tactical in how they think through challenges. They constantly assess their surroundings for anything they can use , even if it's an everyday object that others might ignore . Since rodents are usually prey animals in the food chain, they must be especially vigilant and more aware of their surroundings for quick escapes. Because of their ingenuity, resourceful rodents must be smart enough to use their cunning and high intellect to escape danger and outwit their stronger enemies . Their foes usually tower over them and must be climbed by the rodents to attack the weak point . The rodents must also catch their foes off-guard so they could restrain them and tie them down . Rodents use their inventions to survive and utilize whatever they can find to defend themselves from others . They use the smallest scraps of the environment for protection , turn broken toys into vehicles and inventions , and must think ahead to outmaneuver predators or rivals. Resourceful rodents learn from each encounter with a foe, improving their tactics, developing new techniques, and creating brand-new strategies to outwit the next opponent. The resourceful rodent can fit any definition; a Genius Bruiser / Badass Bookworm who fights with whatever they can get their hands on, The Engineer who invents, rebuilds, and repurposes items from junk to fight foes, and the sly and underhanded rogue who uses their resourcefulness to make shivs , hastily and stealthily evade enemies, and catch foes off-guard . As highly social and empathetic animals, large groups of rodents will show the importance of cooperation and problem-solving when they work as a team to outsmart larger threats and achieve their goals. Resourceful Rodents are not strictly anthropomorphic; a trained rodent, an intelligent rodent, and even an entire group of rodents who create strategies and hunting tactics to take on larger prey can also fit here. Rodents under the control of The Beastmaster also apply because the rodents use methods of their own to follow the beastmaster's orders note for example the beastmaster might order the rats to get them jewelry, the rodents follow this command by creatively stealing someone else's jewelry to give it to their master, whether they wanted stolen jewelry or not. . Alternatively, the rats aren't under any form of control and are just playing along for food and protection. It can go with Bamboo Technology , Improbable Weapon User , Improvisational Ingenuity , MacGyvering , and Scavenged Punk since the animal can display ingenuity by repurposing junk as tools, weapons, transportation, or inventions. It also goes with Guile Hero and The Trickster since the rodent must defeat larger enemies vastly stronger than them. The resourceful rodent is the finesse to lab rat and researcher rodent s' sorcery or technology. This has some elements of being Truth in Television . In real life, rats are very intelligent and social creatures; they are also scavengers who take whatever food they can get. In one experiment in 2016, rats were observed to use tools to access food, but could not use them to their full capabilities due to their poor eyesight. As small prey animals, rodents must rely on caution and stealth to avoid enemies much stronger and larger than themselves. See also Intelligent Gerbil , Nice Mice , Screwball Squirrel , You Dirty Rat! , and Suicidal Lemmings . If the rats are half-human, then it's Rat Men . Rodents of Unusual Size may apply if the animal fits the criteria for the resourceful rodent. If the mice use their intelligence for mischief, deserved or not, then A Mischief of Mice is involved It may overlap with Mouse World , which often involves rodent characters repurposing human objects as tools. It may also overlap with Scavenged Punk (and therefore averting Scavengers Are Scum ) since it can involve rodent characters using materials scavenged or stolen from humans. It may or may not be The Lab Rat (literally), which does the forensics and other research. See also Researcher Rodent , for mice, rats, and more rarely other rodents depicted as being scientists and researchers, and Busy Beaver for beavers, one of the largest rodents in real life , depicted as hardworking for their resourcefulness. Compare Clever Crows for another type of scavenging animals portrayed as intelligent.
